body:
I'm opening this WIP PR (we don't need to merge) because I am investigating the effects of 3rd-order upwinding alone, since #702 introduces more changes: usage of ClimaTimeStepper, 3rd-order-upwinding, addition of continuity equation. I am investigating why the need for a smaller time step there, why the simulation goes unstable, and decided to do one incremental change only here.

By only switching to 3rd-order upwinding in this test case, keeping resolution and time step the same, we incur in errors. 


- [x] Code follows the [style guidelines](https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/) OR N/A.
- [x] Unit tests are included OR N/A.
- [x] Code is exercised in an integration test OR N/A.
- [x] Documentation has been added/updated OR N/A.
title: Deformation flow: 3rd-order upwinding

body:
This PR adds NC support for single column meshes (via `RectilinearMesh`). This should allow us to export nc files in ClimaAtmos for the regression tests in single column cases.

title: Add NC support for single column mesh
